channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Room-temperature high-efficiency spin injection via van der Waals tunnel contact
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67989-7

Researchers developed a simple, industry-compatible method using an indium buffer layer between cobalt and 2D materials to form vdWs tunnel contacts. This achieved high spin injection efficiency in both graphene and MoS2, enabling future large-scale spintronic devices.
item.dc:title: Room-temperature high-efficiency spin injection via van der Waals tunnel contact
item.dc:creator: Shiming Huang
Fuchen Hou
Tingyu Qu
Lianying Zhu
Zhipeng Wang

Guowen Yuan

Huolin Huang
Feng Zhang

Rong Zhang

item.title: Elasto-magnetic instabilities for amplified actuation and mechanical memory
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-68225-y

Here the authors utilize magnetic attraction and elastic tension to create instabilities that amplify vibrations and induce inertia-driven hysteresis. The design enables efficient mechanical memory with both volatile and non-volatile modes.
item.dc:title: Elasto-magnetic instabilities for amplified actuation and mechanical memory
item.dc:creator: Seong-Yu Choi
Ji-Sung Park
Won Jun Song
Maga Kim
Yun Hyeok Lee
Yong Eun Cho
Hakjun Lee
Ho-Young Kim
Jeong-Yun Sun

item.title: A self-assembled molecule directs ordered α-FAPbI<sub>3</sub> for n-i-p perovskite solar cells
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-68214-1

Formamidinium lead iodide perovskite holds promise for high-efficiency solar cells, but the kinetic instability of its α-phase crystallization remains an issue. He et al. report a strategy for directing the crystallization process in a n-i-p architecture and champion power conversion efficiency of 26.18%.
item.dc:title: A self-assembled molecule directs ordered α-FAPbI<sub>3</sub> for n-i-p perovskite solar cells
item.dc:creator: Qingyun He
Junbo Wang
Mengyang Wu
Chongyu Zhong
Lei Li
Xiangru Zhao
Mengzhu Ding
Pinghui Yang
Renzhi Li
Wei Huang
Tianshi Qin
Fangfang Wang

item.title: Regular-wrinkling tunable MXene lattice for electromagnetic interference shielding
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-68035-2

This study reports self-wrinkling MXene films with lattice structures achieving exceptional electromagnetic interference shielding (81.5 dB at 17 μm), offering ultra-thin protection for sensitive electronics.
item.dc:title: Regular-wrinkling tunable MXene lattice for electromagnetic interference shielding
item.dc:creator: Shan Zhang
Juntao Wu
Zhi-Ling Hou
Pengfei Hu
Bo Cai
